site.brandDescription: A Brooklyn wood-fired trattoria serving blistered pies, seasonal antipasti, and easy neighborhood hospitality.

locations.title: Ember & Slice Brooklyn
locations.description: A Brooklyn wood-fired trattoria built around blistered sourdough pies, bright antipasti, and an open oven that runs from lunch through late dinner.
locations.shortDescription: Wood-fired pizza, seasonal antipasti, and warm neighborhood hospitality in Brooklyn.

locations.title: Ember & Slice West Village

locations.shortDescription: Wood-fired pizza, seasonal antipasti, and neighborhood hospitality in the West Village.

siteContent.content: Book a table near the oven.
A trattoria shaped by the oven.

Ember & Slice started with a sourdough starter, a borrowed mixer, and a pop-up oven behind a Brooklyn wine bar. The pies sold out before sunset, then again the next weekend, and then every weekend after that.

Today the room is permanent, but the promise is the same: slow dough, live fire, seasonal produce, and the kind of service that makes a weeknight feel like an occasion.

We cold-ferment our dough, stretch every pie to order, and cook it hot enough for a crisp rim and a tender center. The menu changes around the market, but the Margherita never leaves the board.

The oven anchors the room. Everything else moves around it.

Come for a quick counter pie, stay for antipasti and another round, or bring a group and let the table fill itself. Ember & Slice is casual by design, but the details matter.

Good tomatoes. Good flour. Good fire. No shortcuts.
Pizza classes, tasting nights, and big-table evenings.
Book a hands-on pizza class, a natural wine pairing night, or a family-style evening built around the oven.

experiences.title: Pizza Making Class
experiences.tagline: Stretch dough, top your pie, and fire it yourself.
experiences.body:
Our flagship pizza making class brings guests right up to the bench and oven. You will learn how we stretch our dough, build a balanced pie, and work with high-heat live fire without feeling rushed.

Each booking includes dough, toppings, one personal pizza, and a glass of house wine or sparkling lemonade. Great for couples, visitors, and anyone who wants a hands-on dinner plan in Brooklyn.
experiences.availableNote: Thursday to Sunday. Advance booking recommended.
experiences.seoTitle: Pizza Making Class Brooklyn | Ember & Slice
experiences.seoDescription: Book a hands-on pizza making class at Ember & Slice in Brooklyn. Stretch dough, build your pie, and fire it in our oven.

experiences.title: Natural Wine & Pizza Night
experiences.tagline: Small pours, hot pies, and long-table energy.
experiences.body:
This evening is part tasting, part dinner party. We pair a rotating lineup of natural wines with off-menu pies, seasonal antipasti, and a little background on why each pairing works.

Best for date nights, visiting friends, and anyone who wants the room at its loudest and warmest. Seats are shared at the table, and the menu changes with the week.
experiences.seoTitle: Natural Wine & Pizza Night Brooklyn | Ember & Slice
experiences.seoDescription: Reserve Natural Wine & Pizza Night at Ember & Slice in Brooklyn for curated pours, seasonal pies, and a long-table dinner.

experiences.title: Family Pizza Night
experiences.tagline: Big-table dinner, easy pacing, and pizza for all ages.
experiences.body:
Family Pizza Night is our easiest way to turn a Sunday dinner into something a little more memorable. Kids shape mini pies, grown-ups share large-format pizzas and salads, and the kitchen keeps the pacing relaxed.

Ideal for families, birthday dinners, and mixed-age groups who want an experience that feels special without feeling formal.
experiences.availableNote: Sundays only. Best for groups of 4 to 6.
experiences.seoTitle: Family Pizza Night Brooklyn | Ember & Slice
experiences.seoDescription: Book Family Pizza Night at Ember & Slice in Brooklyn for a relaxed group dinner built around the oven.

reviews.content: The Margherita had that perfect leopard-spotted crust and the basil hit the table smelling fresh. Exactly what I want from a neighborhood pizza night.
Sat at the counter and watched the oven all night. Pepperoni Calabrese, burrata, and a spritz made this feel like a tiny vacation.
reviews.ownerReply: Thank you Julian. The counter seats are our favorite too - come back for the Funghi Bianco next time.

reviews.content: Great crust, warm service, and the garlic knots vanished before the pizza landed. It gets loud at peak dinner but in a good way.
reviews.ownerReply: Thanks Priya. Dinner definitely has energy, and we are glad the knots did their job.

reviews.content: The hot honey soppressata is ridiculous. Sweet, spicy, smoky, and somehow still balanced. Best pie I have had in Williamsburg this year.
Lovely date-night spot without feeling precious. Caesar was sharp and cold, pizza was blistered, staff knew the menu well.
Food was strong but our table was about 15 minutes late on a busy Friday. I would come earlier next time.
reviews.ownerReply: Hi Chris - sorry for the Friday wait. We tightened our turn times and would love to host you again on a smoother night.

reviews.content: Unbelievable sourdough pizza right in the West Village! The Margherita is simple, fresh, and perfectly charred. Truly a hidden gem.
reviews.ownerReply: Thank you Michael! We are thrilled you enjoyed the neighborhood vibes and our signature crust.

reviews.content: Beautiful space, exceptionally friendly service, and a fantastic corner view. Highly recommend the Burrata and the Hot Honey pie!

locationQa.question: Do you take reservations?
locationQa.answer: Yes. We hold room for walk-ins, but reservations are recommended for dinner and weekends.

locationQa.question: Do you offer gluten-free crust?
locationQa.answer: Not yet. Our dough room uses wheat flour all day, so we cannot safely guarantee a gluten-free crust.

locationQa.question: Can I order takeout?
locationQa.answer: Yes. Call us directly for pickup. Wood-fired pies travel best when picked up close to oven time.

locationQa.question: What are your busiest times?
locationQa.answer: Friday and Saturday from 7pm to 9pm are peak. Earlier dinner or Sunday lunch is calmer.

locationQa.question: Do you have vegetarian options?
locationQa.answer: Absolutely. Margherita, Funghi Bianco, Burrata, Garlic Knots, and Rigatoni Pomodoro are vegetarian.

locationQa.question: Do you offer outdoor seating?
locationQa.answer: Absolutely! We have a lovely patio setup for the warmer months.

locationQa.question: Do you offer gluten-free crust?
locationQa.answer: Yes, we offer gluten-free crust for any of our wood-fired pizzas for an additional charge.

posts.title: Weekend lunch now starts at 11
posts.body: The oven is lighting up earlier on Saturdays and Sundays. Come by for lunch pies, garlic knots, and spritzes from 11am.
Our Funghi Bianco is back with roasted mushrooms, ricotta crema, thyme, and a little pecorino snow at the pass.

posts.title: Margherita Monday
posts.body: Every Monday in May: Margherita pies are $14 from open to close. Dine-in only, one per guest.
